title: Native categorical boosting with ordered statistics and explicit crossed IDs
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality `Driver` and many sparse `Race/Year` combinations.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality `Driver` and many sparse `Race/Year` combinations. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics were designed specifically to reduce prediction shift from categorical leakage, which is relevant in this strongly autocorrelated setup.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost on mostly raw columns plus lightweight numerics, keeping `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, `Driver x Race`, `Driver x Year`, and `Race x Compound` as native categorical features. Compare against the current manual frequency/median encoding under grouped chronological CV.
expected_effect: May unlock interaction signal the manual preprocessing is flattening, particularly for rare drivers/tracks and sparse compound usage patterns.
risk: If folds are not grouped chronologically by driver-race, ordered statistics can still look better offline than on leaderboard; CatBoost may also underperform if numerics already dominate.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
